6.0.description: Create attached gridpoints. Gridpoints on one zone may be rigidly attached (or slaved) to master gridpoints, edges, or faces of other zones. This allows grids that do not exactly topologically match to behave as a single grid. Each gridpoint so attached results in the creation of an internal data structure (an Attach Point) that is accessible by command and by FISH.

keywords.name: delete
keywords.description: Removes attach conditions on all slaved gridpoints within the given range. All attach conditions are removed if no range is present.

keywords.name: by-face
keywords.description: Searches the mesh for all surface faces in the range. For this purpose a surface face is defined to be a face that is not perfectly topologically connected to a zone on the other side. For faces that are coplanar and touching (within a tolerance), attach conditions are automatically generated between gridpoints on one side and faces on the other. The side with the smaller faces will be selected to be attached (the slave).

keywords.name: by-face-snap
keywords.description: If snap is set to on (the default), then turns slave gridpoint locations are moved to correspond to the locations they are slaved to on the master, both immediately and during cycling. If snap is set to off, slave gridpoints will move along with the master relatively, but their positions might be different.

keywords.name: by-face-tolerance-absolute
keywords.description: If specified, f is the maximum absolute distance between the slave gridpoint and the target face for an attach condition to be created. The default value is 0.0, meaning a relative tolerance scheme is used instead.

keywords.name: by-face-tolerance-angle
keywords.description: Faces are attached only if the angle between them is less than f in degrees. By default this value is 5 degrees. It is sometimes necessary to increase this tolerance for complex surfaces that create non-planar faces.

keywords.name: by-face-tolerance-relative
keywords.description: Gridpoints are attached to faces only if the distance is within a distance of f times the absolute value of the position of the face centroid. The default value is 1x10-5. It may be necessary to increase this value if there was additional error in grid creation. [CS: check this out; usage here is fuzzy (past tense? when “was” the error]

keywords.name: gridpointid
keywords.description: Attaches a single gridpoint with ID number ids to: another gridpoint, an edge between two gridpoints, or a zone face. One of the following keywords is required to indicate the target of the attach condition.

keywords.name: gridpointid-to-gridpointid
keywords.description: Attaches the gridpoint to the gridpoint with ID idm. By default snap is on and will cause gridpoint ids to move to the same location as gridpoint idm, both immediately and during cycling.

keywords.name: gridpointid-to-edgeids
keywords.description: The gridpoint with ID number ids is attached to the edge defined as the line between gridpoints i1 and i2.

keywords.name: gridpointid-to-edgeids-weight
keywords.description: The optional keyword weight can be given to define the location of gridpoint ids between gridpoints i1 and i2. If weight = 0.0, then ids is located at i1; if weight = 1.0, then ids is located at i2. If not specified, this weighting is calculated automatically based on the position of gridpoint ids.

keywords.name: gridpointid-to-edgeids-snap
keywords.description: By default snap is on and will cause gridpoint ids to move to the location given by the weighting between i1 and i2 automatically, both immediately and during cycling.

keywords.name: gridpointid-to-faceid
keywords.description: The gridpoint with ID number ids is attached to the face identified as side iside of zone with ID idz. URGENT: CS: section referred to here appears to be absent (Refer to:flag2:-Command Reference Section 1.1.4 Orientation of Nodes and Faces within a Zone-).]

keywords.name: gridpointid-to-faceid-weight
keywords.description: The optional keyword weight can be given to define the location of gridpoint ids on face idz, iside1 with respect to the face vertices. Each of the four value given must be between 0.0 and 1.0, and the sum should add to 1.0 (assuming the slave location is on the face). For example, the location of the first vertex would correspond to 1.0, 0.0, 0.0, 0.0, and the center of the face would correspond to 0.25, 0.25, 0.25, 0.25.

keywords.name: gridpointid-to-faceid-snap
keywords.description: By default snap is on and will cause gridpoint ids to move to the location given by the weighting on zone face idz, iside1 automatically, both immediately and during cycling.

keywords.name: group-slot
keywords.description: Assign the group to slot s.

keywords.name: group-remove
keywords.description: Remove the group s from all attach conditions in the range. If slot is not specified, it will be removed from all slots it is found in.

keywords.name: list
keywords.description: List information on all attach conditions in the range. This information includes the slave gridpoints, the master targets, and the weighting factors if appropriate.

keywords.description: The gridpoint with ID number ids is attached to the face identified as side iside of zone with ID idz. URGENT: CS: section referred to here appears to be absent (Refer to:flag2:-Command Reference Section 1.1.4 Orientation of Nodes and Faces within a Zone-).]

keywords.description: The optional keyword weight can be given to define the location of gridpoint ids on face idz, iside1 with respect to the face vertices. Each of the four value given must be between 0.0 and 1.0, and the sum should add to 1.0 (assuming the slave location is on the face). keywords.name: group-slot
keywords.description: Assign the group to slot s.

keywords.name: group-remove
keywords.description: Remove the group s from all attach conditions in the range. If slot is not specified, it will be removed from all slots it is found in.

keywords.name: list
keywords.description: List information on all attach conditions in the range. This information includes the slave gridpoints, the master targets, and the weighting factors if appropriate.

keywords.name: delete
keywords.description: Remove attach conditions on gridpoints in the range, or all attach conditions if no range is supplied.

keywords.name: list
keywords.description: List all attach conditions in effect (or those touching gridpoints in the range).

keywords.name: gp
keywords.description: Short form for `gridpointid <i>` -- identifies the slave gridpoint by id.

keywords.name: by-face
keywords.description: Create attach conditions by automatically matching coincident faces across zone groups. Sub-keywords: snap, tolerance-absolute, tolerance-angle, tolerance-relative.

keywords.name: by-face-snap
keywords.description: Snap matched gridpoints to a common location instead of attaching with weights.

keywords.name: by-face-tolerance-absolute
keywords.description: Absolute distance tolerance for face matching.

keywords.name: by-face-tolerance-angle
keywords.description: Angle tolerance (in degrees) for face-normal matching.

keywords.name: by-face-tolerance-relative
keywords.description: Relative distance tolerance (fraction of face edge length) for face matching.

keywords.name: gridpointid
keywords.description: Attach the slave gridpoint with id i to another gridpoint, an edge, or a face. Sub-keywords: to-gridpointid, to-edgeids, to-faceid, gp (alias for to-gridpointid).

keywords.name: gridpointid-to-gridpointid
keywords.description: Attach the slave gridpoint to the master gridpoint with id idm. Optional snap b snaps to a coincident location instead of attaching with unit weight.

keywords.name: gridpointid-to-edgeids
keywords.description: Attach the slave gridpoint to the edge between gridpoints id1 and id2. Sub-keywords: weight f, snap b.

keywords.name: gridpointid-to-edgeids-weight
keywords.description: Interpolation weight along the edge (0 = id1, 1 = id2).

keywords.name: gridpointid-to-edgeids-snap
keywords.description: Snap the slave gridpoint to the interpolated edge point.

keywords.name: gridpointid-to-faceid
keywords.description: Attach the slave gridpoint to face iside of zone idz. Sub-keywords: weight f1 f2 f3 f4, snap b.

keywords.name: gridpointid-to-faceid-weight
keywords.description: Interpolation weights for the four (or three) face corner gridpoints.

keywords.name: gridpointid-to-faceid-snap
keywords.description: Snap the slave gridpoint to the interpolated face point.

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: Assign group s to attach points in the range. Sub-keywords: slot s, remove.

keywords.name: group-slot
keywords.description: Slot the group is assigned to (default 'Default').

keywords.name: group-remove
keywords.description: Remove the named group from attach points in the range.
